primaryTitle: Father of Ibrahim — Named in the Quran
full: Āzar ibn Nāhūr ibn Sārūgh
titles: Azar · Terah (biblical parallel) · father of Ibrahim
school: Father of Ibrahim; the only father of a prophet named directly in the Quran (6:74). An idol-worshipper whom Ibrahim tried to guide to monotheism; his refusal became a defining trial of Ibrahim's mission. Mentioned four times in the Quran.

books.title: Quran — Sura al-An'am (6:74)
books.note: Ibrahim's address to his father Azar about idol worship

city: Ur of the Chaldees / Canaan / Mecca
titles: Khalil Allah · Abu al-Anbiya · Hanif · Ibrahim al-Khalil
school: One of the five greatest prophets (Ulu al-Azm); father of monotheism; builder of the Ka'ba with Ismail; progenitor of both the Arab (through Ismail) and Israelite (through Ishaq) prophetic lines; model of complete submission to God

books.title: Quran — Sura Ibrahim (14)
books.note: The sura named for Ibrahim, containing his supplications

primaryTitle: Dhabih Allah — The Sacrifice of God, Father of the Arabs
full: Ismā'īl ibn Ibrāhīm ibn Āzar
titles: Ismail · Dhabih Allah · Abu al-'Arab · Sayyid al-'Arab
school: First-born son of Ibrahim; helped build the Ka'ba in Mecca; ancestor of the Arab peoples and of Prophet Muhammad; known as Dhabih Allah for his willingness to be sacrificed; a prophet sent to the people of ancient Arabia

books.title: Quran — Sura al-Saffat (37:101–113)
books.note: The account of the sacrifice of Ismail

full: 'Adnān
titles: Adnan · progenitor of the Adnanite Arabs · ancestor of Prophet Muhammad
school: The furthest point in Muhammad's lineage that classical Muslim scholars consider historically reliable. Ibn Ishaq explicitly instructed scholars not to go beyond Adnan in claiming certainty, as the chain between Ismail and Adnan spans many unverifiable generations. Universally accepted ancestor of the northern (Adnanite) Arab tribes.

primaryTitle: Eponymous Ancestor of the Quraysh Tribe
full: Fihr ibn Mālik ibn al-Nadr al-Qurashī
titles: Fihr · Quraysh (the tribe takes its name from him) · ancestor of Prophet Muhammad
school: The ancestor after whom the Quraysh tribe is named in the dominant genealogical tradition. The Quraysh were the guardians of the Ka'ba and the noblest tribe in Arabia. All early caliphs and the Prophet himself descended from Fihr. The name 'Quraysh' derives from the Arabic root meaning to gather or concentrate — a reference to Fihr's role consolidating his people.

primaryTitle: Reunifier of the Quraysh — Founder of Meccan Governance
full: Qusayy ibn Kilāb ibn Murra al-Qurashī
titles: Qusayy · Mujammi' (the Gatherer) · great-great-grandfather of Muhammad
school: First historically well-attested ancestor of Muhammad. He seized control of Mecca from the Khuza'a tribe and united the Quraysh clans under his leadership. He built the Dar al-Nadwa (the council house of Mecca), established the custodianship of the Ka'ba in Quraysh, and created the civic offices of Mecca that endured until the Islamic era. Ibn Ishaq calls him the first great leader of the Quraysh.

books.title: Sirat Rasul Allah (Ibn Ishaq / Ibn Hisham)
books.note: Account of Qusayy's conquest of Mecca and establishment of Quraysh rule

primaryTitle: Ancestor of Prophet Muhammad — in the chain from Adnan to Prophet Muhammad
full: 'Abd Manāf ibn Qusayy
school: Listed in the prophetic lineage between Adnan and Prophet Muhammad in Ibn Ishaq's Sira and al-Tabari's Tarikh. No independent biographical record survives beyond the genealogical chain.

books.title: Tarikh al-Tabari
books.note: Primary Islamic source for pre-Islamic genealogical chains

primaryTitle: Founder of Banu Hashim — Feeder of the Pilgrims
full: Hāshim ibn 'Abd Manāf al-Qurashī
titles: Hashim · 'Amr al-'Ula · founder of the Hashimite clan · died in Gaza on trade
school: Great-grandfather of Prophet Muhammad and founder of the Banu Hashim clan of the Quraysh. He established the two great Meccan trade caravans — the winter journey to Yemen and summer journey to Syria — referenced directly in Sura Quraysh (106). He fed pilgrims during the Hajj, a role so celebrated that his name Hashim means 'the one who crumbles bread'. He died in Gaza during a trading journey to Syria.

books.title: Quran — Sura Quraysh (106)
books.note: The winter and summer caravans established by Hashim ibn Abd Manaf

primaryTitle: Grandfather of the Prophet — Keeper of the Zamzam Well
full: Shayba ibn Hāshim, known as 'Abd al-Muṭṭalib al-Hāshimī
classif: Tribal Chief / Guardian of the Prophet
titles: Abd al-Muttalib · Shayba al-Hamd · guardian of the Ka'ba · raised the Prophet Muhammad
school: Grandfather and guardian of Prophet Muhammad. He rediscovered and redug the Zamzam well after receiving a divine dream directing him to its location. He raised Muhammad after the deaths of his father Abdullah and mother Amina. He led the Quraysh during Abraha's invasion — the Year of the Elephant (570 CE) in which the Ka'ba was miraculously protected. He died when Muhammad was approximately eight years old.

books.title: Tarikh al-Tabari
books.note: Account of Abd al-Muttalib, the Zamzam well, and the Year of the Elephant

primaryTitle: Father of Prophet Muhammad
full: 'Abd Allāh ibn 'Abd al-Muṭṭalib al-Hāshimī
titles: Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib · father of Prophet Muhammad
school: Father of Prophet Muhammad. He married Amina bint Wahb of the Banu Zuhra. He died in Medina during or returning from a trading journey to Syria, either shortly before or just after Muhammad's birth, making the Prophet an orphan from the start. The Quran references this: 'Did He not find you an orphan and shelter you?' (93:6).

books.title: Quran — Sura al-Duha (93:6)
books.note: God's reminder to Muhammad of his orphanhood

primaryTitle: Prophet of the 'Ad — Messenger to Ancient Arabia
full: Hūd ibn Shalikh ibn Arfakhshadh
titles: Hud · Prophet of the 'Ad people · mentioned 7 times in the Quran
school: Sent to the 'Ad people of ancient Arabia; preached monotheism against idol worship; his people were destroyed by a violent wind after rejecting him; a Quranic sura bears his name

books.title: Quran — Sura Hud (11)
books.note: Contains the full account of Hud and the 'Ad

primaryTitle: Prophet of Thamud — Messenger of the Rock-Hewn City
full: Ṣāliḥ ibn 'Ubayd ibn Māshij
titles: Salih · Prophet of Thamud · associated with the miraculous she-camel
school: Sent to the Thamud people who carved their homes from rock at Al-Hijr (modern Mada'in Salih in Saudi Arabia); the miraculous she-camel was his sign; his people were destroyed by a thunderbolt after killing the camel

books.title: Quran — Sura al-A'raf (7:73–79)
books.note: The story of Salih and Thamud

primaryTitle: Prophet of Sodom — Messenger to the Cities of the Plain
full: Lūṭ ibn Hārān ibn Tārikh
titles: Lut · nephew of Ibrahim · prophet to the people of Sodom
school: Nephew of Ibrahim; sent to the people of Sodom (Qawm Lut); preached against their moral corruption; his people were destroyed; one of the most frequently mentioned prophets in the Quran

books.title: Quran — Sura Hud (11:77–83)
books.note: The full account of Lut and the destruction of his people

primaryTitle: Prophet of the Israelites — Second Son of Ibrahim
full: Isḥāq ibn Ibrāhīm ibn Āzar
titles: Ishaq · Isaac · son of Ibrahim and Sarah · father of Yaqub
school: Son of Ibrahim and Sarah; father of Yaqub and grandfather of Yusuf; progenitor of the Israelite prophetic line; mentioned 17 times in the Quran; blessed by God as a prophet from birth

books.title: Quran — Sura al-Anbiya (21:72)
books.note: Mention of Ishaq as a gift to Ibrahim

primaryTitle: Isra'il — Father of the Twelve Tribes
full: Ya'qūb ibn Isḥāq ibn Ibrāhīm
titles: Yaqub · Israel (Isra'il) · son of Ishaq · father of the twelve tribes
school: Son of Ishaq, grandson of Ibrahim; given the name Isra'il; father of the twelve sons who became the progenitors of the twelve tribes of Israel, including Yusuf; prominent in the account of Yusuf in Sura Yusuf

books.title: Quran — Sura Yusuf (12)
books.note: Yaqub features prominently in the story of his son Yusuf

primaryTitle: Ahsan al-Qasas — The Most Beautiful of Stories
full: Yūsuf ibn Ya'qūb ibn Isḥāq ibn Ibrāhīm
titles: Yusuf · Joseph the Beautiful · Siddiq (the Truthful) · Vizier of Egypt
school: Son of Yaqub; his life story is described in the Quran as 'the most beautiful of stories'; sold into slavery by his brothers, rose to become Vizier of Egypt; known for his beauty, patience, wisdom, and ability to interpret dreams; a model of forgiveness

books.title: Quran — Sura Yusuf (12)
books.note: The only Quranic sura dedicated entirely to one prophet's story

primaryTitle: Khatib al-Anbiya — The Eloquent of the Prophets
full: Shu'ayb ibn Mikāyil ibn Yashjur
titles: Shu'ayb · Khatib al-Anbiya · father-in-law of Musa in some traditions
school: Prophet sent to the people of Midian; preached against dishonest trade, fraud in weights and measures, and idol worship; considered by some traditions to be the father-in-law of Musa; his people were destroyed when they rejected him

books.title: Quran — Sura al-A'raf (7:85–93)
books.note: The mission of Shu'ayb to the people of Midian

primaryTitle: Sabir — The Patient, Model of Endurance
full: Ayyūb ibn Maws ibn Rā'il ibn 'Isāw
titles: Ayyub · Job · al-Sabir · model of patience and gratitude amid suffering
school: Quranic prophet celebrated above all for his extraordinary patience through severe affliction; after years of suffering he called upon God and was healed and restored; his story is the supreme Quranic model of patient endurance (sabr) and trust in God

books.title: Quran — Sura al-Anbiya (21:83–84)
books.note: Ayyub's supplication and restoration

primaryTitle: Man of the Pledge — Patient and Righteous Prophet
full: Dhū al-Kifl
titles: Dhul-Kifl · possibly identified with Ezekiel or Elisha · man of the double portion
school: Mentioned twice in the Quran alongside Ismail and Idris as being among the patient and righteous; identified by some scholars with the biblical Ezekiel; his name (Dhul-Kifl, 'man of the pledge') may refer to a covenant he fulfilled

books.title: Quran — Sura al-Anbiya (21:85–86)
books.note: Brief Quranic mention of Dhul-Kifl among the patient

primaryTitle: Wazir Musa — Minister and Brother of Moses
full: Hārūn ibn 'Imrān
titles: Harun · Aaron · brother and minister of Musa · Wazir al-Nabi
school: Elder brother of Musa; appointed by God as a prophet and minister (wazir) to assist Musa; known for his eloquence; became the first High Priest of the Israelites; his story is inseparable from that of Musa in the Quranic narrative

books.title: Quran — Sura Ta-Ha (20:29–36)
books.note: Musa's prayer to God to appoint Harun as his helper

primaryTitle: Kalim Allah — He Who Spoke Directly with God
full: Mūsā ibn 'Imrān
titles: Musa · Kalim Allah · Khatim al-Anbiya al-Isra'iliyyin · Moses · Kalimu'llah
school: One of the five greatest prophets (Ulu al-Azm); most frequently mentioned prophet in the Quran; led the Israelites out of Egypt; received the Torah on Mount Sinai; spoke directly with God without intermediary; the supreme model of divine communication and social liberation

books.title: Quran — Sura Ta-Ha (20)
books.note: A complete retelling of Musa's mission

books.title: Quran — Sura al-Qasas (28)
books.note: The story of Musa from birth through his mission

primaryTitle: Khalifa of God on Earth — Prophet-King and Psalmist
full: Dāwūd ibn Ayshu (Jesse)
titles: Dawud · David · Khalifat Allah · recipient of the Zabur · slayer of Jalut (Goliath)
school: Prophet-King of Israel; recipient of the Zabur (Psalms); given wisdom and sovereignty; slew the giant Jalut (Goliath) as a young man; his voice in glorification of God caused mountains and birds to join him; father of Sulayman; established Jerusalem as the holy city

books.title: Quran — Sura Saba' (34:10–11)
books.note: God's gifts to Dawud: softening iron and the glorification of mountains

primaryTitle: Malik al-Anbiya — The King of the Prophets
full: Sulaymān ibn Dāwūd
titles: Sulayman · Solomon · builder of Bayt al-Maqdis · master of jinn and wind
school: Son of Dawud; given unparalleled dominion over humans, jinn, animals, and the wind; builder of the Temple in Jerusalem; known for his extraordinary wisdom; the Queen of Sheba (Bilqis) converted under his influence; a whole sura of the Quran is named for the ants (al-Naml) in his story

books.title: Quran — Sura al-Naml (27:15–44)
books.note: The story of Sulayman, the hoopoe, and the Queen of Sheba

primaryTitle: Prophet of Carmel — Reformer Against Idol Worship
full: Ilyās ibn Yasīn ibn Fanhas ibn al-'Ayzār ibn Hārūn
titles: Ilyas · Elijah · taken up to heaven without death according to tradition
school: Prophet of the northern Israelite kingdom; fought against idol worship (Baal) at Mount Carmel; praised in the Quran as among the righteous; his identity with the mysterious figure of Khidr is debated in Islamic tradition; known for miracles and fiery zeal for monotheism

books.title: Quran — Sura al-Saffat (37:123–132)
books.note: The Quranic account of Ilyas among the messengers

primaryTitle: Successor of Ilyas — The Chosen Prophet
full: Al-Yasa' ibn Akhtub ibn al-'Ajuz
titles: Al-Yasa' · Elisha · spiritual successor of Ilyas · chosen above the worlds
school: Prophet of the Israelite kingdom; disciple and spiritual successor of Ilyas; mentioned twice in the Quran as one whom God chose above all others; known in biblical tradition for extraordinary miracles including reviving the dead; carried on Ilyas's mission of monotheism

books.title: Quran — Sura al-An'am (6:86)
books.note: Al-Yasa' listed among the prophets guided by God

primaryTitle: Dhul-Nun — The Man of the Whale
full: Yūnus ibn Mattā
titles: Yunus · Dhul-Nun (Man of the Whale) · Jonah · Sahib al-Hut
school: Prophet sent to Nineveh; left his mission prematurely and was swallowed by a great fish; called upon God from within darkness with the prayer of Dhul-Nun (Tasbih Yunus); his supplication from the belly of the whale is among the most powerful prayers in the Quran; his people repented en masse

books.title: Quran — Sura Yunus (10)
books.note: The sura named for Yunus

books.title: Quran — Sura al-Anbiya (21:87–88)
books.note: Yunus's prayer from within the whale: La ilaha illa Anta

primaryTitle: Guardian of Maryam — Prophet of the Temple
